top_weaknesses.issue: Two paragraphs over 80 words (82 + 85) under 'What it doesn't buy' — dense on mobile and the section where abstraction is highest
top_weaknesses.fix: Split each at the natural pivot ('Open-weight releases compete...' / 'Cheaper AI is not the same as more egalitarian AI.')

top_weaknesses.issue: H2s ('What the gap proves,' 'What the leak buys you,' 'What it doesn't buy,' 'The forward question') are abstract and metaphor-dependent — weak standalone for skimmers and search
top_weaknesses.fix: Sharpen with entity nouns: e.g., 'What DeepSeek's $5.6M Actually Proves,' 'What the Open-Weight Leak Buys CTOs,' 'What It Doesn't Buy: Chips and Cloud,' 'Which Curve to Bet On'

top_weaknesses.issue: Voice drifts toward consultant/op-ed in macro sections (labor-share, China/export-controls); abstract noun density rises ('substrate remains concentrated,' 'capability gates,' 'permanent rent')
top_weaknesses.fix: Cut the labor-share paragraph or tighten to one line; add one concrete deployment example (a real CTO swap, a real workload migration)

top_weaknesses.issue: Anchor text could be more specific ('2 to 3x per year' could be 'frontier AI compute growth 2-3x per year'; '64% in 1974 to roughly 56% by 2024' is data-only)
top_weaknesses.fix: Tighten anchors to name the source/concept explicitly

consensus_summary: Sharp framing and a quotable closing line ('the frontier moves up while the cost-to-replicate-last-year's-frontier moves down'); thesis is HN-shareable. Held back by abstract H2s, two long paragraphs, and a macro/labor section that drifts into op-ed voice.
